text.title: Golden Set Curation and Maintenance Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Seed coverage across at least 4 buckets: happy_path, edge_case, adversarial, known_failure_class. Distribution target ≥15% per bucket. Pure happy-path sets rejected.
rule.rationale: Happy-path-only sets pass with 95%+ on broken models because the broken behavior only shows under load or weird inputs.

rule.statement: Every golden item must carry an anti_output field: ≥1 plausible-wrong output the model should NOT produce.
rule.rationale: Anti-outputs catch 'right answer, wrong reason' regressions and improve LLM-judge calibration.

rule.statement: Every production incident involving the AI feature MUST produce ≥1 golden-set candidate within 7 days of postmortem. Track conversion ratio (target ≥80%).
rule.rationale: Incidents are pre-labelled, free, high-value data points. Skipping conversion repeats the same bug class quarterly.

rule.statement: Every 90 days, audit each item against current production behavior. Tag still_valid / schema_changed / retire. Track retired-count.
rule.rationale: Golden sets degrade ~10-20% per quarter through silent business-rule drift.

rule.statement: New items enter via PR with: dataset_version bump, complete per-item metadata, coverage report attached, ≥1 reviewer outside the proposing author.
rule.rationale: Unversioned datasets cause silent regressions; reviewer requirement catches biased labels from the author.

rule.statement: Skip for pre-prototype unstable schema, pure exploratory research, creative-content output (no consensus correctness), or when an existing RAG-eval framework already enforces golden-set discipline.
rule.rationale: Stable schema is the precondition for stable golden labels; creative outputs don't have ground truth.
